items.title: Brands: 'Geen vertrekclausules in nieuw contract van Bosz bij PSV'
items.summary: Peter Bosz heeft geen vertrekclausules opgenomen in zijn nieuwe contract bij PSV . Hoewel de coach daar zelf niets over wilde zeggen, bevestigde algemeen directeur Marcel Brands dat desgevraagd.

items.title: Geen Lyon: Dallinga kiest voor kansen bij Bologna
items.summary: Thijs Dallinga (25) blijft deze winter bij Bologna. De aanvaller was in verregaande gesprekken met Olympique Lyon, maar de transfer is niet doorgezet. Dat bevestigen ingewijden.

items.title: Transfers PSV: Stewart verheugd met contractverlenging Bosz
items.summary: VI volgt het transfernieuws bij PSV op de voet, zeker nu de winterse transferwindow in aantocht is. De doelwitten van de Eindhovenaren, duiding van de clubwatcher, of aandacht in buitenlandse media. We verzamelen alles hier.

items.title: Szoboszlai: Liverpool future 'not in my hands'
items.summary: Liverpool midfielder Dominik Szoboszlai has admitted he would love to extend his stay at the club but said the decision is "not in [his] hands".

items.summary: ⚽️ Updates from the 4.30pm (GMT) kick-off ⚽️ Live scores | Full table | Follow on Bluesky | Mail Dominic An email from Spurs fan SF Devereux: As well as those eight you mentioned (Maddison, Kulusevski, Kudus, van de Ven, Bentancur, Bergvall, Porro and Davies), we’re also without Richarlison, Kevin Danso, Djed Spence and Wilson Odobert. We just need a goalkeeper to go down injured today and we’ll have a pretty competitive, tactically balanced XI missing, with a couple of options available to come off the bench. Continue reading...

items.title: Manchester City v Chelsea: Women’s Super League – live
items.summary: ⚽️ Updates from the 2.30pm (GMT) kick-off at the Etihad ⚽️ Live scores | Full table | Follow on Bluesky | Mail Emillia After six consecutive years as champions, Chelsea find their once firm grip on the Women’s Super League crown has been reduced to a little finger clinging to the side of the trophy. They head to the Etihad Stadium on Sunday nine points behind their opponents and surely sensing that only a win could prevent the title from transferring to Manchester City’s outstretched arms. The big surprise here is the absence of Lucy Bronze for Chelsea. She has a ‘minor injury’, we are told. That’s a massive blow for the visitors. Manchester City, meanwhile, are overjoyed to welcome back Mary Fowler after her lengthy injury absence. Continue reading...

items.title: Aston Villa’s title hopes dealt blow by 10-man Brentford amid VAR drama
items.summary: Aston Villa were already seething at trailing to 10-man Brentford and then, four minutes into the second half, those frustrations grew tenfold. Tammy Abraham thought he had made a goalscoring return, his first since winning promotion from the Championship seven years ago, but then it was all eyes on the referee, Tim Robinson, and the big screens that indicated a review. At Stockley Park, the video assistant referee, Paul Tierney, rewound the clock by 19 seconds, to the moment Leon Bailey, at the opposite end of the pitch, was deemed to have failed to keep the ball in play. After a VAR delay of almost four minutes, Robinson’s mic connected with the stadium speakers. “After review, the ball is factually out of play, so the restart will be a throw-in to Brentford,” the referee said amid mass groans – and much worse – from the restless locals. The Premier League’s Match Centre said the ball was out of play “within the attacking possession phase (APP)”. Continue reading...

items.title: Bosz verbindt toekomst aan PSV met nieuw contract
items.summary: Peter Bosz heeft zijn toekomst aan PSV verbonden. De 62-jarige oefenmeester heeft zijn aflopende contract bij de landkampioen met twee jaar verlengd.

items.title: Ten-man Nottingham Forest earn valuable point in feisty clash with Crystal Palace
items.summary: Neco Williams was a very relieved man at the final whistle after his Nottingham Forest teammates held on against Crystal Palace to move a point further away from the relegation zone. The full-back produced a fantastic save and some amateur dramatics but was dismissed, having given away a penalty, in a game his side looked favourites to win. Ismaïla Sarr scoring the resulting spot-kick to cancel out Morgan Gibbs-White’s opener was the only additional damage, as Forest endured an afternoon on the backfoot. If it was not for Palace currently living in striker purgatory, waiting for one to leave and a replacement to arrive, they may have made more of the one-man advantage, rather than extend their winless run to nine matches. Continue reading...

items.title: VI Live: Jans kijkt met FC Utrecht alweer tegen een achterstand aan in Heerenveen
items.summary: Welkom in de VI Live van zondag 1 februari. Hier nemen we je mee in drie Eredivisie-duels, een topper in Engeland en alle andere hoogtepunten op de (inter)nationale velden.

items.title: Sesko seals late Man United win in 5-goal thriller...
items.summary: Benjamin Sesko scored a last-minute winner as Manchester United overcame Fulham in a five-goal thriller at Old Trafford on Sunday.

items.title: Mbappé scores last-gasp pen as Madrid edge Rayo
items.summary: Kylian Mbappé's penalty in the 10th minute of stoppage time secured a dramatic 2-1 victory for Real Madrid over lowly Rayo Vallecano to cut Barcelona's lead at the top of LaLiga.

items.title: Sources: Liverpool go for Sunderland's Geertruida
items.summary: Liverpool are interested in signing Netherlands international Lutsharel Geertruida on loan from RB Leipzig, sources have told ESPN.
